brief_justification: Correct answer (Yes) with valid causal reasoning; Multi-level analysis covering causal structure, backdoor paths, collider bias, and method comparison across 4 explicit steps; Some self-monitoring via suppression flags and noting the correlation≠causation instinct before overriding it, but no deep bias reflection; Basic verification by checking both methods converge but no counterfactual or boundary-condition test; Acknowledges the correlation-causation distinction and the closed-world assumption but doesn't quantify confidence; Evaluates both methods and explains why each works but doesn't consider alternative causal structures; Complete derivation chain from DAG specification through backdoor criterion to conclusion, fully reproducible.
